commands.review_summary:
Adds Codex app-server developer guidance to read listed `SKILL.md` paths directly and a lifecycle test asserting that wording.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction is established. Source inspection shows current main lacks the direct-read developer instruction while docs define skills as listed file paths, but I did not run a hidden or gitignored state-directory Codex app-server turn.
commands.review_followup: This should stay on the normal maintainer/contributor review path because it has a protected label and the remaining blocker is real behavior proof, not a repairable code defect.

target.title: fix(codex): read listed skill files directly
target.body:
Summary:
- Add Codex app-server guidance to read listed `SKILL.md` paths directly instead of searching parent directories.
- Cover the prompt guidance in the Codex app-server thread lifecycle tests.

Verification:
- `pnpm install`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts`

Real behavior proof:
Behavior addressed: Codex app-server prompts no longer encourage parent-directory skill discovery that can fail under hidden or gitignored OpenClaw/Codex state directories.
Real environment tested: Local macOS Codex worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: The targeted test asserts that developer instructions tell Codex to read listed skill files directly and not search parent directories.
Observed result after fix: The targeted test file passed with 37 tests.
What was not tested: Full test suite and a live Codex app-server Slack turn.
target.author: kevinslin
